name: CIS Critical Security Controls
description: The CIS Critical Security Controls are a prioritized set of actions that collectively form a defense-in-depth set of best practices to mitigate the most common attacks against systems and networks.

categories.name: Implementation Group 1 - Basic
categories.description: Essential cyber hygiene and foundational controls for all organizations.

controls.name: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
controls.description: Actively manage all enterprise assets connected to the infrastructure, physically, virtually, remotely, and those within cloud environments.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain detailed enterprise asset inventory.
requirements.guidance: Use automated tools to maintain an accurate inventory of all assets. Include information such as network address, hardware address, machine name, enterprise asset owner, department, and whether the asset is approved to connect.

requirements.description: Address unauthorized assets.
requirements.guidance: Ensure that a process exists to address unauthorized assets on a weekly basis. Remove from the network, deny remote access, or quarantine the asset.

requirements.description: Utilize an active discovery tool.
requirements.guidance: Use an active discovery tool to identify assets connected to the network. Configure the tool to execute daily or more frequently.

requirements.description: Use DHCP logging to update enterprise asset inventory.
requirements.guidance: Use DHCP logging on all DHCP servers or IP address management tools to update the enterprise asset inventory.

controls.name: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
controls.description: Actively manage all software on the network so that only authorized software is installed and can execute.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a software inventory.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a detailed inventory of all licensed software installed on enterprise assets. Include title, publisher, initial install date, and business purpose.

requirements.description: Ensure authorized software is currently supported.
requirements.guidance: Ensure that only authorized software is installed. Address unauthorized software at least monthly.

requirements.description: Address unauthorized software.
requirements.guidance: Ensure that unauthorized software is either removed or the inventory is updated in a timely manner.

requirements.description: Use allowlisting for software.
requirements.guidance: Use technical controls to ensure only authorized software can execute on assets.

controls.name: Data Protection
controls.description: Develop processes and technical controls to identify, classify, securely handle, retain, and dispose of data.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a data management process.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a data management process including data sensitivity levels, data owner, data retention, and disposal requirements.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a data inventory.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a data inventory based on data sensitivity levels. Review and update at least annually.

requirements.description: Configure data access control lists.
requirements.guidance: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

requirements.description: Enforce data retention.
requirements.guidance: Retain data according to data management process. Data retention must include minimum and maximum retention periods.

requirements.description: Encrypt data on end-user devices.
requirements.guidance: Encrypt data on end-user devices using full-disk encryption or file-level encryption.

controls.name: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.description: Establish and maintain the secure configuration of enterprise assets and software.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a secure configuration process.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a secure configuration process for enterprise assets and software. Review and update at least annually.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a secure configuration process for network infrastructure.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a secure configuration process for network infrastructure such as routers, switches, and firewalls.

requirements.description: Configure automatic session locking.
requirements.guidance: Configure automatic session locking on enterprise assets after a defined period of inactivity.

requirements.description: Implement and manage a firewall on servers.
requirements.guidance: Implement and manage a firewall on servers. Configure to deny traffic by default.

requirements.description: Securely manage enterprise assets and software.
requirements.guidance: Securely manage assets and software. Use network-based management protocols such as SSH, HTTPS, or vendor-specific protocols.

requirements.description: Manage default accounts on enterprise assets and software.
requirements.guidance: Manage default accounts on enterprise assets and software. Disable or rename them when possible.

controls.name: Account Management
controls.description: Use processes and tools to assign and manage authorization to credentials for user accounts.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain an inventory of accounts.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain an inventory of all accounts managed in the enterprise. Include user, administrator, and service accounts.

requirements.description: Use unique passwords.
requirements.guidance: Use unique passwords for all enterprise assets. Best practice is using a password manager.

requirements.description: Disable dormant accounts.
requirements.guidance: Delete or disable any dormant accounts after a period of 45 days of inactivity.

requirements.description: Restrict administrator privileges to dedicated administrator accounts.
requirements.guidance: Restrict administrator privileges to dedicated administrator accounts on enterprise assets.

controls.name: Access Control Management
controls.description: Use processes and tools to create, assign, manage, and revoke access credentials and privileges for user, administrator, and service accounts.

requirements.description: Establish an access granting process.
requirements.guidance: Establish and follow a process, preferably automated, for granting access to enterprise assets upon new hire, rights transfer, or role change.

requirements.description: Establish an access revoking process.
requirements.guidance: Establish and follow a process, preferably automated, for revoking access to enterprise assets through disabling accounts.

requirements.description: Require MFA for externally-exposed applications.
requirements.guidance: Require multi-factor authentication for externally-exposed enterprise or third-party applications.

requirements.description: Require MFA for remote network access.
requirements.guidance: Require MFA for remote network access to enterprise resources.

requirements.description: Require MFA for administrative access.
requirements.guidance: Require MFA for all administrative access accounts where supported.

controls.name: Continuous Vulnerability Management
controls.description: Develop a plan to continuously assess and track vulnerabilities on all enterprise assets.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a vulnerability management process.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a documented vulnerability management process for enterprise assets. Review and update at least annually.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a remediation process.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a risk-based remediation strategy documented in a remediation process.

requirements.description: Perform automated operating system patch management.
requirements.guidance: Perform operating system updates on enterprise assets through automated patch management.

requirements.description: Perform automated application patch management.
requirements.guidance: Perform application updates on enterprise assets through automated patch management.

controls.name: Audit Log Management
controls.description: Collect, alert, review, and retain audit logs of events that could help detect, understand, or recover from an attack.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain an audit log management process.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain an audit log management process defining log requirements. At a minimum, address collection, review, and retention of audit logs.

requirements.description: Collect audit logs.
requirements.guidance: Collect audit logs. Ensure that logging, per the enterprise audit log management process, is enabled across enterprise assets.

requirements.description: Ensure adequate audit log storage.
requirements.guidance: Ensure that logging destinations maintain adequate storage to comply with the enterprise log management process.

categories.name: Implementation Group 2 - Foundational
categories.description: Additional controls for organizations with moderate resources and cybersecurity expertise.

controls.name: Email and Web Browser Protections
controls.description: Improve protections and detections of threats from email and web vectors.

requirements.description: Ensure use of only fully supported browsers and email clients.
requirements.guidance: Ensure only fully supported browsers and email clients are allowed to execute in the enterprise.

requirements.description: Use DNS filtering services.
requirements.guidance: Use DNS filtering services on all enterprise assets to block access to known malicious domains.

requirements.description: Maintain and enforce network-based URL filters.
requirements.guidance: Enforce and update network-based URL filters to limit enterprise assets from connecting to potentially malicious websites.

requirements.description: Restrict unnecessary or unauthorized browser and email client extensions.
requirements.guidance: Restrict installation of unnecessary browser and email client extensions.

controls.name: Malware Defenses
controls.description: Prevent or control the installation, spread, and execution of malicious applications, code, or scripts on enterprise assets.

requirements.description: Deploy and maintain anti-malware software.
requirements.guidance: Deploy and maintain anti-malware software on all enterprise assets.

requirements.description: Configure automatic anti-malware signature updates.
requirements.guidance: Configure automatic updates for anti-malware signature files on all enterprise assets.

requirements.description: Disable autorun and autoplay for removable media.
requirements.guidance: Disable autorun and autoplay auto-execute functionality for removable media.

requirements.description: Configure automatic anti-malware scanning of removable media.
requirements.guidance: Configure anti-malware software to automatically scan removable media.

controls.name: Data Recovery
controls.description: Establish and maintain data recovery practices sufficient to restore in-scope enterprise assets to a pre-incident and trusted state.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a data recovery process.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a data recovery process that addresses scope, prioritization, and security of backup data.

requirements.description: Perform automated backups.
requirements.guidance: Perform automated backups of in-scope enterprise assets. Run backups weekly or more frequently.

requirements.description: Protect recovery data.
requirements.guidance: Protect recovery data with equivalent controls to the original data. Reference encryption or data separation.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain an isolated instance of recovery data.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain an isolated instance of recovery data using offline, cloud, or off-site systems.

controls.name: Network Infrastructure Management
controls.description: Establish and maintain the secure configuration of network devices.

requirements.description: Ensure network infrastructure is up-to-date.
requirements.guidance: Ensure network infrastructure is kept up-to-date. Example: regularly updating firmware, using up-to-date software.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a secure network architecture.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a secure network architecture. A secure network architecture addresses segmentation, least privilege, and availability.

requirements.description: Securely manage network infrastructure.
requirements.guidance: Securely manage network infrastructure. Example implementations include AAA, network management protocols, and out-of-band management.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain architecture diagram(s).
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain architecture diagram(s) and/or other network system documentation.

controls.name: Network Monitoring and Defense
controls.description: Operate processes and tooling to establish and maintain comprehensive network monitoring and defense against security threats.

requirements.description: Centralize security event alerting.
requirements.guidance: Centralize security event alerting across enterprise assets for log correlation and analysis.

requirements.description: Deploy a host-based intrusion detection solution.
requirements.guidance: Deploy a host-based intrusion detection solution on enterprise assets.

requirements.description: Deploy a network intrusion detection solution.
requirements.guidance: Deploy a network intrusion detection solution on enterprise assets.

requirements.description: Perform traffic filtering between network segments.
requirements.guidance: Perform traffic filtering between network segments as defined in network architecture.

controls.name: Security Awareness and Skills Training
controls.description: Establish and maintain a security awareness program to influence behavior among the workforce to be security conscious.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a security awareness program.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a security awareness program. Update content at least annually or when significant enterprise changes occur.

requirements.description: Train workforce members to recognize social engineering attacks.
requirements.guidance: Train workforce members to recognize social engineering attacks such as phishing, pretexting, and tailgating.

requirements.description: Train workforce members on authentication best practices.
requirements.guidance: Train workforce members on authentication best practices including MFA, password composition, and credential management.

requirements.description: Train workforce on data handling best practices.
requirements.guidance: Train workforce members on data handling best practices. Include data classification, labeling, and handling throughout its lifecycle.

categories.name: Implementation Group 3 - Organizational
categories.description: Advanced controls for organizations with significant resources and cybersecurity expertise.

controls.name: Service Provider Management
controls.description: Develop a process to evaluate service providers who hold sensitive data or are responsible for critical IT platforms or processes.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain an inventory of service providers.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain an inventory of service providers. Include classification of service providers based on data handled and services provided.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a service provider management policy.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a service provider management policy addressing requirements for service provider assessments and contracting.

requirements.description: Classify service providers.
requirements.guidance: Classify service providers based on inherent risk using organizational risk assessment methodology.

requirements.description: Ensure service provider contracts include security requirements.
requirements.guidance: Ensure service provider contracts include security requirements including minimum security program requirements, security incident notification, and data handling.

controls.name: Application Software Security
controls.description: Manage the security life cycle of in-house developed, hosted, or acquired software.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a secure application development process.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a secure application development process addressing security throughout the software development lifecycle.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a process for accepting third-party software.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a process to accept and address reports of software vulnerabilities including a public mechanism for reporting.

requirements.description: Perform root cause analysis on security vulnerabilities.
requirements.guidance: Perform root cause analysis on security vulnerabilities. Identify and address the source of vulnerabilities.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain an inventory of third-party software components.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain an inventory of third-party software components used within the enterprise.

controls.name: Incident Response Management
controls.description: Establish a program to develop and maintain an incident response capability.

requirements.description: Designate personnel to manage incident handling.
requirements.guidance: Designate one key person, and at least one backup, who will manage the enterprise incident handling process.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain contact information for reporting security incidents.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain contact information for parties to contact during a security incident.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain an enterprise process for reporting incidents.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain an enterprise process for the workforce to report security incidents.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain an incident response process.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain an incident response process addressing roles, responsibilities, communication, and phases of incident handling.

requirements.description: Assign key roles and responsibilities.
requirements.guidance: Assign key roles and responsibilities for incident response including staff from legal, IT, information security, facilities, HR, and communications.

requirements.description: Define mechanisms for communicating during incident response.
requirements.guidance: Define mechanisms for communicating during incident response including both internal and external communication methods.

controls.name: Penetration Testing
controls.description: Test the effectiveness and resiliency of enterprise assets through identifying and exploiting weaknesses in controls.

requirements.description: Establish and maintain a penetration testing program.
requirements.guidance: Establish and maintain a penetration testing program appropriate to the size, complexity, and maturity of the enterprise.

requirements.description: Perform periodic external penetration tests.
requirements.guidance: Perform periodic external penetration tests based on program requirements, at least annually.

requirements.description: Remediate penetration test findings.
requirements.guidance: Remediate penetration test findings based on the enterprise policy for remediation scope and prioritization.

requirements.description: Validate security measures.
requirements.guidance: Validate security measures after each penetration test. Ensure findings have been properly addressed.
